{{Quote|Being prepared is our best weapon.|Tol Sivron|<ref name="CotF" />}}
'''Tol Sivron''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] and former member of the [[Head clan (Ryloth)|Head Clan]] of [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] before being exiled. After making his escape from his homeworld, Sivron later became a manager for the [[Imperial Department of Military Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]], and [[Chief Administrator/Legends|Chief Administrator]] of the [[Maw Installation]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Is that in the emergency procedures?|Tol Sivron|<ref name="CotF" />}}
In his early life, Sivron served as a member of the [[Head clan (Ryloth)|Head Clan]] on [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]]. When chosen, it was expected that he would serve in the Head Clan for many years. However, about one year afterward, another member of the Head Clan fell while inspecting a project, and impaled himself. Sivron and the three other members of the Head Clan were then cast out. Sivron convinced the three other members to pool their resources, and try to eke out a living in one of the nearby uninhabited caves.<ref name="CotF" />

While the other former Head Clan members slept, Sivron murdered all of them. He then took their supplies, and set out in a trek across the desert. Eventually he came upon an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] refueling station on Ryloth, where he met a young [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] named [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]. Tarkin soon found that Sivron was an excellent manager of the complex business arrangements that Tarkin had.<ref name="CotF" />

Eventually, Tarkin assigned Tol Sivron as the Chief Administrator of Maw Installation's research facilities. For over ten years, Sivron ran the facility that produced such weapons as the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]] and [[Sun Crusher]].<ref name="CotF" />

When the facility was invaded by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces, Sivron escaped with the other scientists onboard the [[Death Star prototype]] that was in orbit around the facility. He took the weapon to [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], and [[Battle of Kessel (11 ABY)|used]] the superlaser to destroy the planet's [[Garrison Moon]], which proved the effectiveness of the weapon. He [[Battle of the Maw|returned]] to [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]] Installation to destroy it rather than allow it to fall into Rebel hands, however Sivron died when the Death Star prototype fell into a nearby [[Black hole/Legends|black hole]], after he attempted to chase and destroy the stolen [[Sun Crusher]] that had begun firing on the battle station.<ref name="CotF" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Tol Sivron has never had any initiative in his entire life.|[[Natasi Daala]]|<ref name="CotF" />}}
Tol Sivron didn't like making decisions himself and instead preferred to follow procedure to the letter. He was very bureaucratic in his nature and based most of his life around committee meetings. He also never liked to take blame himself and when things went wrong, he would attribute them to the failings of others or with the appropriate procedure, rather than himself.<ref name="CotF" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SivronLemelisk.jpg|thumb|Tol Sivron with [[Bevel Lemelisk]].]]
Tol Sivron was created by author [[Kevin J. Anderson]]. Sivron's rigid following of station protocol is a parody of protocol enforced by the [[Wikipedia:Llnl|Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory]], where Anderson worked for twelve years.<ref name="dwolver">{{WebCite|url=http://www.alternativenation.net/interview-star-wars-author-kevin-j-anderson-recounts-jedi-academy-trilogy-darksaber-tales-jedi|text=Interview: Star Wars Author Kevin J. Anderson On 'Jedi Academy Trilogy', 'Darksaber', & 'Tales of the Jedi'|author=McCausland, Doug|date=2014-11-18|archivedate=20141204180927}}</ref>
